tldr: Burlington's Committee of the Whole approved a temporary two-year development charge exemption for residential construction, adopted a new climate action plan, and passed a comprehensive residential zoning bylaw after extensive public consultation. The meeting spanned two days with significant time spent on policy changes aimed at housing development and environmental goals.

items.title: 2026 Halton Court Services business plan and budget
items.notes: Committee approved the 2026 budget for Halton Court Services as presented in the staff report.

items.title: Reserve fund request to strengthen municipal resilience
items.notes: Authorized transfer of $75,000 from Tax Rate Stabilization reserve fund to support Emergency and Continuity Program.

items.title: Heritage Conservation Agreement for 461 Elizabeth Street
items.notes: Authorized Mayor and City Clerk to sign Heritage Conservation Agreement for Knox Presbyterian Church heritage grant application.

items.title: Heritage Conservation Agreement for 2411 Lakeshore Road
items.notes: Authorized Mayor and City Clerk to sign Heritage Conservation Agreement for heritage grant program application.

items.title: Amendment to Procedure By-law regarding member speaking time
items.notes: Enacted by-law amendment to provide clarity on member speaking time and questions during council meetings.

items.title: Recreation Fee Assistance Program improvements 2025
items.notes: Received information report on improvements made to the Recreation Fee Assistance Program in 2025.

items.title: Motion regarding City of Burlington leaving X social media
items.notes: Directed staff to report back in Q4 2026 on best use of social media platforms and consequences of leaving X platform.

items.title: Amendments to Council Code of Good Governance and Staff Relations Policy
items.notes: Directed comprehensive review of staff council relations policy including Strong Mayor Powers provisions and Council Code of Good Governance alignment with regional standards.

items.title: 2026 Lakeshore Music & Arts Festival event overview
items.notes: Received festival information report and directed staff to support a community-driven parade concurrent with the festival.

items.title: Contingency Reserve status report
items.notes: Received confidential information report on Contingency Reserve status as of December 31, 2025.

items.title: E-Scooter Micromobility Pilot Program update
items.notes: Received information report on the status and results of the E-Scooter Micromobility Pilot Program.

items.title: Burlington Climate Plan: Taking Action to Reduce Community Greenhouse Gas Emissions
items.notes: Approved Burlington Climate Action Plan to reduce community greenhouse gas emissions after hearing from environmental groups.

items.title: Burlington Avenue and Ontario Street Heritage Conservation District Plan
items.notes: Approved by-law to designate study area as Heritage Conservation District and adopted the conservation district plan.

items.title: Heritage response to Bill 23 - Phase II shortlist of designation candidates
items.notes: Directed staff to retain consultant to assess eligibility of 10 properties for potential heritage designation under Ontario Heritage Act.

items.title: Options for temporary elimination of Development Charges
items.notes: Approved two-year residential Development Charges exemption for units with building permits showing active construction, despite failed amendments for provincial funding guarantees.

items.title: New Residential Zoning Bylaw - Final
items.notes: Approved and enacted comprehensive new Burlington Residential Zoning By-laws after statutory public meeting with extensive delegation input from developers, realtors, and community groups.

items.title: Information Items - Various correspondence and materials
items.notes: Received 15 information items including legislative forecasts, delegation materials, and correspondence related to various agenda items.
